raw_consumer_statement:
I reached out to Citi to report a fraudulent business transaction with a furniture company called XXXX on XX/XX/year>XXXX   when I was unable to connect with any of the contact information listed on the receipt and previous information listed on this furniture company. The parent company is XXXX which is located at XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX, CA XXXX. I ordered a custom furniture in the amount of {$6500.00} after multiple contacts with their sales rep, XXXX XXXX, over the phone, email and text messages. I finally purchase a custom made living room set and received a receipt and confirmation of the payment. In the last email I received from them, they provided instruction that it will take 62 days to complete the custom furnitures and I shall receive another email for shipping and delivery instructions. I never received shipping/ delivery emails. I tried to reach back to XXXX and XXXX XXXX however, the phone numbers, email addresses and even their website have been taken down. Thus, I could not reach anyone. It was this time that I sent a transaction dispute with Citi ( XX/XX/year>XXXX  ) explaining what have happened. They stated that it would take up to 90 days to complete their investigation and in the meantime, I would receive a conditional credit. I just received their completed/ resolved investigation on XX/XX/year>. In their letter, they stated the following : " Through our investigation, of the information and facts available, we were unable to identify or determine that a billing error occurred. 
We previously issued a conditional credit to your account for the amount in question pending the outcome of our investigation. Now that the investigation is complete, we have reversed the conditional credit. All adjustments will appear on your stated within 2 billing periods. '' I do not understand the language in their dispute letter as it it very vague. XXXX is definitely gone with my money and I have no other way to contact the furniture company to get the custom furniture I ordered. It did not state whether they were able to reach out and connect with the company so the goods/ furnitures I ordered will be delivered at a certain timeline. There is no process to appeal Citi 's " resolved '' dispute process.
